children.heading: Treatment as effectively connected with United States trade or business
children.label: 1 Treatment as effectively connected with United States trade or business
A
children.text: in the case of a nonresident alien individual, under section 871(b)(1), or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a foreign corporation, under section 882(a)(1),

children.heading: Minimum tax on nonresident alien individuals
children.label: 2 Minimum tax on nonresident alien individuals
i
children.text: the individual’s alternative minimum taxable income (as defined in section 55(b)(1)(D)) for the taxable year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual’s net United States real property gain for the taxable year.

children.heading: Net United States real property gain
children.label: B Net United States real property gain
i
children.text: the aggregate of the gains for the taxable year from dispositions of United States real property interests, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate of the losses for the taxable year from dispositions of such interests.

children.heading: Limitation on losses of individuals
children.label: b Limitation on losses of individuals
children.text: In the case of an individual, a loss shall be taken into account under subsection (a) only to the extent such loss would be taken into account under section 165(c) (determined without regard to subsection (a) of this section).

children.heading: United States real property interest
children.label: c United States real property interest
i
children.text: an interest in real property (including an interest in a mine, well, or other natural deposit) located in the United States or the Virgin Islands, and

children.label: I
children.text: the period after , during which the taxpayer held such interest, orJune 18, 1980

children.label: II
children.text: the 5-year period ending on the date of the disposition of such interest.

children.heading: Exclusion for interest in certain corporations
children.label: B Exclusion for interest in certain corporations
i
children.text: as of the date of the disposition of such interest, such corporation did not hold any United States real property interests,

children.label: I
children.text: were disposed of in transactions in which the full amount of the gain (if any) was recognized, or

children.label: II
children.text: ceased to be United States real property interests by reason of the application of this subparagraph to 1 or more other corporations, and

children.label: iii
children.text: neither such corporation nor any predecessor of such corporation was a regulated investment company or a real estate investment trust at any time during the shorter of the periods described in subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: United States real property holding corporation
children.label: 2 United States real property holding corporation
A
children.text: the fair market value of its United States real property interests equals or exceeds 50 percent of

children.label: i
children.text: its United States real property interests,

children.label: ii
children.text: its interests in real property located outside the United States, plus

children.label: iii
children.text: any other of its assets which are used or held for use in a trade or business.

children.heading: Exception for stock regularly traded on established securities markets
children.label: 3 Exception for stock regularly traded on established securities markets
children.text: If any class of stock of a corporation is regularly traded on an established securities market, stock of such class shall be treated as a United States real property interest only in the case of a person who, at some time during the shorter of the periods described in paragraph (1)(A)(ii), held more than 5 percent of such class of stock.

children.heading: Interests held by foreign corporations and by partnerships, trusts, and estates
children.label: 4 Interests held by foreign corporations and by partnerships, trusts, and estates

children.heading: Foreign corporations
children.label: A Foreign corporations
children.text: Paragraph (1)(A)(ii) shall be applied by substituting “any corporation (whether foreign or domestic)” for “any domestic corporation”.

children.heading: Assets held by partnerships, etc.
children.label: B Assets held by partnerships, etc.
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, assets held by a partnership, trust, or estate shall be treated as held proportionately by its partners or beneficiaries. Any asset treated as held by a partner or beneficiary by reason of this subparagraph which is used or held for use by the partnership, trust, or estate in a trade or business shall be treated as so used or held by the partner or beneficiary. Any asset treated as held by a partner or beneficiary by reason of this subparagraph shall be so treated for purposes of applying this subparagraph successively to partnerships, trusts, or estates which are above the first partnership, trust, or estate in a chain thereof.

children.label: i
children.text: the stock which the first corporation holds in the second corporation shall not be taken into account,

children.label: ii
children.text: the first corporation shall be treated as holding a portion of each asset of the second corporation equal to the percentage of the fair market value of the stock of the second corporation represented by the stock held by the first corporation, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any asset treated as held by the first corporation by reason of clause (ii) which is used or held for use by the second corporation in a trade or business shall be treated as so used or held by the first corporation.

children.heading: Controlling interest
children.label: B Controlling interest
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “controlling interest” means 50 percent or more of the fair market value of all classes of stock of a corporation.

children.heading: Interest in real property
children.label: A Interest in real property
children.text: The term “interest in real property” includes fee ownership and co-ownership of land or improvements thereon, leaseholds of land or improvements thereon, options to acquire land or improvements thereon, and options to acquire leaseholds of land or improvements thereon.

children.heading: Real property includes associated personal property
children.label: B Real property includes associated personal property
children.text: The term “real property” includes movable walls, furnishings, and other personal property associated with the use of the real property.

children.heading: Constructive ownership rules
children.label: C Constructive ownership rules
children.text: For purposes of determining under paragraph (3) whether any person holds more than 5 percent of any class of stock and of determining under paragraph (5) whether a person holds a controlling interest in any corporation, section 318(a) shall apply (except that paragraphs (2)(C) and (3)(C) of section 318(a) shall be applied by substituting “5 percent” for “50 percent”).

children.heading: Treatment of distributions by foreign corporations
children.label: d Treatment of distributions by foreign corporations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except to the extent otherwise provided in regulations, notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, gain shall be recognized by a foreign corporation on the distribution (including a distribution in liquidation or redemption) of a United States real property interest in an amount equal to the excess of the fair market value of such interest (as of the time of the distribution) over its adjusted basis.

children.label: i
children.text: at the time of the receipt of the distributed property, the distributee would be subject to taxation under this chapter on a subsequent disposition of the distributed property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the basis of the distributed property in the hands of the distributee is no greater than the adjusted basis of such property before the distribution, increased by the amount of gain (if any) recognized by the distributing corporation, or

children.label: B
children.text: if such nonrecognition is provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary under subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except to the extent otherwise provided in subsection (d) and paragraph (2) of this subsection, any nonrecognition provision shall apply for purposes of this section to a transaction only in the case of an exchange of a United States real property interest for an interest the sale of which would be subject to taxation under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: the extent to which nonrecognition provisions shall, and shall not, apply for purposes of this section, and

children.label: i
children.text: transfers of property in reorganization, and

children.label: ii
children.text: changes in interests in, or distributions from, a partnership, trust, or estate,

children.heading: Nonrecognition provision defined
children.label: 3 Nonrecognition provision defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “nonrecognition provision” means any provision of this title for not recognizing gain or loss.

children.heading: Special rule for sales of interest in partnerships, trusts, and estates
children.label: g Special rule for sales of interest in partnerships, trusts, and estates
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, the amount of any money, and the fair market value of any property, received by a nonresident alien individual or foreign corporation in exchange for all or part of its interest in a partnership, trust, or estate shall, to the extent attributable to United States real property interests, be considered as an amount received from the sale or exchange in the United States of such property.

children.heading: Special rules for certain investment entities
children.label: h Special rules for certain investment entities

children.heading: Look-through of distributions
children.label: 1 Look-through of distributions
children.text: Any distribution by a qualified investment entity to a nonresident alien individual, a foreign corporation, or other qualified investment entity shall, to the extent attributable to gain from sales or exchanges by the qualified investment entity of United States real property interests, be treated as gain recognized by such nonresident alien individual, foreign corporation, or other qualified investment entity from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, any distribution by a qualified investment entity to a nonresident alien individual or a foreign corporation with respect to any class of stock which is regularly traded on an established securities market located in the United States shall not be treated as gain recognized from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest if such individual or corporation did not own more than 5 percent of such class of stock at any time during the 1-year period ending on the date of such distribution.

children.heading: Sale of stock in domestically controlled entity not taxed
children.label: 2 Sale of stock in domestically controlled entity not taxed
children.text: The term “United States real property interest” does not include any interest in a domestically controlled qualified investment entity.

children.heading: Distributions by domestically controlled qualified investment entities
children.label: 3 Distributions by domestically controlled qualified investment entities
children.text: In the case of a domestically controlled qualified investment entity, rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) shall apply to the foreign ownership percentage of any gain.

children.label: i
children.text: any real estate investment trust, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any regulated investment company which is a United States real property holding corporation or which would be a United States real property holding corporation if the exceptions provided in subsections (c)(3) and (h)(2) did not apply to interests in any real estate investment trust or regulated investment company.

children.heading: Domestically controlled
children.label: B Domestically controlled
children.text: The term “domestically controlled qualified investment entity” means any qualified investment entity in which at all times during the testing period less than 50 percent in value of the stock was held directly or indirectly by foreign persons.

children.heading: Foreign ownership percentage
children.label: C Foreign ownership percentage
children.text: The term “foreign ownership percentage” means that percentage of the stock of the qualified investment entity which was held (directly or indirectly) by foreign persons at the time during the testing period during which the direct and indirect ownership of stock by foreign persons was greatest.

children.label: i
children.text: the period beginning on , and ending on the date of the disposition or of the distribution, as the case may be,June 19, 1980

children.label: ii
children.text: the 5-year period ending on the date of the disposition or of the distribution, as the case may be, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the period during which the qualified investment entity was in existence.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of any class of stock of the qualified investment entity which is regularly traded on an established securities market in the United States, a person holding less than 5 percent of such class of stock at all times during the testing period shall be treated as a United States person unless the qualified investment entity has actual knowledge that such person is not a United States person,

children.label: I
children.text: any class of stock of which is regularly traded on an established securities market, or

children.label: II
children.text: which is a regulated investment company which issues redeemable securities (within the meaning of section 2 of the Investment Company Act of 1940),

children.label: iii
children.text: any stock in the qualified investment entity held by any other qualified investment entity not described in subclause (I) or (II) of clause (ii) shall only be treated as held by a United States person in proportion to the stock of such other qualified investment entity which is (or is treated under clause (ii) or (iii) as) held by a United States person.

children.heading: Treatment of certain wash sale transactions
children.label: 5 Treatment of certain wash sale transactions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If an interest in a domestically controlled qualified investment entity is disposed of in an applicable wash sale transaction, the taxpayer shall, for purposes of this section, be treated as having gain from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest in an amount equal to the portion of the distribution described in subparagraph (B) with respect to such interest which, but for the disposition, would have been treated by the taxpayer as gain from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest under paragraph (1).

children.label: I
children.text: disposes of an interest in a domestically controlled qualified investment entity during the 30-day period preceding the ex-dividend date of a distribution which is to be made with respect to the interest and any portion of which, but for the disposition, would have been treated by the taxpayer as gain from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest under paragraph (1), and

children.label: II
children.text: acquires, or enters into a contract or option to acquire, a substantially identical interest in such entity during the 61-day period beginning with the 1st day of the 30-day period described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Application to substitute dividend and similar payments
children.label: ii Application to substitute dividend and similar payments
I
children.text: any substitute dividend payment (within the meaning of section 861), or

children.label: II
children.text: any other similar payment specified in regulations which the Secretary determines necessary to prevent avoidance of the purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Exception where distribution actually received
children.label: iii Exception where distribution actually received
children.text: A transaction shall not be treated as an applicable wash sales transaction if the nonresident alien individual, foreign corporation, or qualified investment entity receives the distribution described in clause (i)(I) with respect to either the interest which was disposed of, or acquired, in the transaction.

children.heading: Exception for certain publicly traded stock
children.label: iv Exception for certain publicly traded stock
children.text: A transaction shall not be treated as an applicable wash sales transaction if it involves the disposition of any class of stock in a qualified investment entity which is regularly traded on an established securities market within the United States but only if the nonresident alien individual, foreign corporation, or qualified investment entity did not own more than 5 percent of such class of stock at any time during the 1-year period ending on the date of the distribution described in clause (i)(I).

children.heading: Election by foreign corporation to be treated as domestic corporation
children.label: i Election by foreign corporation to be treated as domestic corporation
A
children.text: a foreign corporation holds a United States real property interest, and

children.label: B
children.text: under any treaty obligation of the United States the foreign corporation is entitled to nondiscriminatory treatment with respect to that interest,

children.heading: Revocation only with consent
children.label: 2 Revocation only with consent
children.text: Any election under paragraph (1), once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: if all of the owners of all classes of interests (other than interests solely as a creditor) in the foreign corporation at the time of the election consent to the making of the election and agree that gain, if any, from the disposition of such interest after , which would be taken into account under subsection (a) shall be taxable notwithstanding any provision to the contrary in a treaty to which the United States is a party, andJune 18, 1980

children.label: B
children.text: subject to such other conditions as the Secretary may prescribe by regulations with respect to the corporation or its shareholders.

children.heading: Exclusive method of claiming nondiscrimination
children.label: 4 Exclusive method of claiming nondiscrimination
children.text: The election provided by paragraph (1) shall be the exclusive remedy for any person claiming discriminatory treatment with respect to this section, section 1445, and section 6039C.
the fair market value of such property transferred, over

children.label: A
children.text: the adjusted basis of such property in the hands of the transferor, plus

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of gain, if any, recognized to the transferor under any other provision at the time of the transfer.

children.heading: Special rules relating to real estate investment trusts
children.label: k Special rules relating to real estate investment trusts

children.heading: Increase in percentage ownership for exceptions for persons holding publicly traded stock
children.label: 1 Increase in percentage ownership for exceptions for persons holding publicly traded stock

children.heading: Dispositions
children.label: A Dispositions
children.text: In the case of any disposition of stock in a real estate investment trust, paragraphs (3) and (6)(C) of subsection (c) shall each be applied by substituting “more than 10 percent” for “more than 5 percent”.

children.heading: Distributions
children.label: B Distributions
children.text: In the case of any distribution from a real estate investment trust, subsection (h)(1) shall be applied by substituting “10 percent” for “5 percent”.

children.heading: Stock held by qualified shareholders not treated as United States real property interest
children.label: 2 Stock held by qualified shareholders not treated as United States real property interest
i
children.text: stock of a real estate investment trust which is held directly (or indirectly through 1 or more partnerships) by a qualified shareholder shall not be treated as a United States real property interest, and

children.label: ii
children.text: notwithstanding subsection (h)(1), any distribution to a qualified shareholder shall not be treated as gain recognized from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest to the extent the stock of the real estate investment trust held by such qualified shareholder is not treated as a United States real property interest under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: subparagraph (A)(i) shall not apply to the applicable percentage of the stock of the real estate investment trust held by the qualified shareholder, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the applicable percentage of the amounts realized by the qualified shareholder with respect to any disposition of stock in the real estate investment trust or with respect to any distribution from the real estate investment trust attributable to gain from sales or exchanges of a United States real property interest shall be treated as amounts realized from the disposition of United States real property interests.

children.heading: Special rule for certain distributions treated as sale or exchange
children.label: C Special rule for certain distributions treated as sale or exchange
i
children.text: in the case of an applicable investor, subparagraph (B) shall apply with respect to such distribution, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of any other person, such distribution shall be treated under section 857(b)(3)(F) as a dividend from a real estate investment trust notwithstanding any other provision of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: holds an interest (other than an interest solely as a creditor) in such qualified shareholder, and

children.label: ii
children.text: holds more than 10 percent of the stock of such real estate investment trust (whether or not by reason of the person’s ownership interest in the qualified shareholder).

children.heading: Constructive ownership rules
children.label: E Constructive ownership rules
children.text: For purposes of subparagraphs (B)(i) and (D), the constructive ownership rules under subsection (c)(6)(C) shall apply.

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: F Applicable percentage
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), the term “applicable percentage” means the percentage of the value of the interests (other than interests held solely as a creditor) in the qualified shareholder held by applicable investors.

children.label: I
children.text: is eligible for benefits of a comprehensive income tax treaty with the United States which includes an exchange of information program and the principal class of interests of which is listed and regularly traded on 1 or more recognized stock exchanges (as defined in such comprehensive income tax treaty), or

children.label: II
children.text: is a foreign partnership that is created or organized under foreign law as a limited partnership in a jurisdiction that has an agreement for the exchange of information with respect to taxes with the United States and has a class of limited partnership units which is regularly traded on the New York Stock Exchange or Nasdaq Stock Market and such class of limited partnership units value is greater than 50 percent of the value of all the partnership units,

children.label: ii
children.text: is a qualified collective investment vehicle, and

children.label: iii
children.text: maintains records on the identity of each person who, at any time during the foreign person’s taxable year, holds directly 5 percent or more of the class of interest described in subclause (I) or (II) of clause (i), as the case may be.

children.label: I
children.text: is eligible for benefits under the comprehensive income tax treaty described in subparagraph (A)(i)(I), but only if the dividends article of such treaty imposes conditions on the benefits allowable in the case of dividends paid by a real estate investment trust, and

children.label: II
children.text: is eligible under such treaty for a reduced rate of withholding with respect to ordinary dividends paid by a real estate investment trust even if such person holds more than 10 percent of the stock of such real estate investment trust,

children.label: I
children.text: is a publicly traded partnership (as defined in section 7704(b)) to which subsection (a) of section 7704 does not apply,

children.label: II
children.text: is a withholding foreign partnership for purposes of chapters 3, 4, and 61, and

children.label: III
children.text: if such foreign partnership were a domestic corporation, would be a United States real property holding corporation (determined without regard to paragraph (1)) at any time during the 5-year period ending on the date of disposition of, or distribution with respect to, such partnership’s interests in a real estate investment trust, or

children.label: I
children.text: fiscally transparent within the meaning of section 894, or

children.label: II
children.text: required to include dividends in its gross income, but entitled to a deduction for distributions to persons holding interests (other than interests solely as a creditor) in such foreign person.

children.label: i
children.text: such partner’s distributive share of the amount of gain taken into account under subsection (a)(1) by the partner for the taxable year (determined without regard to this paragraph) shall be increased by the amount of such excess, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such partner’s distributive share of items of income or gain for the taxable year that are not treated as gain taken into account under subsection (a)(1) (determined without regard to this paragraph) shall be decreased (but not below zero) by the amount of such excess.

children.label: I
children.text: any gain recognized from the disposition of a United States real property interest, and

children.label: II
children.text: any distribution by a real estate investment trust that is treated as gain recognized from the sale or exchange of a United States real property interest, over

children.label: ii
children.text: any loss recognized from the disposition of a United States real property interest.

children.heading: Proportionate share of USRPI gain
children.label: C Proportionate share of USRPI gain
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, an applicable investor’s proportionate share of USRPI gain shall be determined on the basis of such investor’s share of partnership items of income or gain (excluding gain allocated under section 704(c)), whichever results in the largest proportionate share. If the investor’s share of partnership items of income or gain (excluding gain allocated under section 704(c)) may vary during the period such investor is a partner in the partnership, such share shall be the highest share such investor may receive.

children.heading: Exception for qualified foreign pension funds
children.label: l Exception for qualified foreign pension funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, a qualified foreign pension fund shall not be treated as a nonresident alien individual or a foreign corporation. For purposes of the preceding sentence, an entity all the interests of which are held by a qualified foreign pension fund shall be treated as such a fund.

children.label: A
children.text: which is created or organized under the law of a country other than the United States,

children.label: i
children.text: by such country (or one or more political subdivisions thereof) to provide retirement or pension benefits to participants or beneficiaries that are current or former employees (including self-employed individuals) or persons designated by such employees, as a result of services rendered by such employees to their employers, or

children.label: ii
children.text: by one or more employers to provide retirement or pension benefits to participants or beneficiaries that are current or former employees (including self-employed individuals) or persons designated by such employees in consideration for services rendered by such employees to such employers,

children.label: C
children.text: which does not have a single participant or beneficiary with a right to more than five percent of its assets or income,

children.label: D
children.text: which is subject to government regulation and with respect to which annual information about its beneficiaries is provided, or is otherwise available, to the relevant tax authorities in the country in which it is established or operates, and

children.label: i
children.text: contributions to such trust, corporation, organization, or arrangement which would otherwise be subject to tax under such laws are deductible or excluded from the gross income of such entity or arrangement or taxed at a reduced rate, or

children.label: ii
children.text: taxation of any investment income of such trust, corporation, organization or arrangement is deferred, or such income is excluded from the gross income of such entity or arrangement or is taxed at a reduced rate.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection.
